name: Route of the Castles
name_original: Ruta de los Castillos – circular desde Villarejo de Salvanés hasta Fuentidueña del Tajo – gravel
description: Cycle the Route of the Castles, a 25.3-mile moderate gravel touring route from Villarejo de Salvanés to Fuentidueña del Tajo, exploring hist
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villarejo de Salvanés offers diverse landscapes, from picturesque hills and valleys to extensive farmlands. The region features vast olive groves and pine forests, including the natural Aleppo pine forest of Valdepuerco. Many routes utilize unpaved roads and dedicated greenways, such as the Tajuña Greenway, providing varied terrain for cyclists. This area provides panoramic views and a mix of cultivated fields and natural features. Best touring cycling routes around Villarejo De Salvanés The most popular touring cycling route is Route of the Castles, a 25.3 miles (40.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This route allows exploration of historical sites and varied landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Tajuña Greenway – Cantarranas Mill Hotel loop from Villarejo de Salvanés, a moderate 33.3 miles (53.6 km) path. This route passes through the Tajuña Greenway, known for its varied landscapes and greenways. Local touring cyclists also love the Chinchón – Chinchón Castle loop from Villarejo de Salvanés, a 33.4 miles (53.8 km) trail leading through historical sites and the picturesque main square of Chinchón, often completed in about 4 hours 31 minutes. Touring cycling around Villarejo de Salvanés is defined by olive groves, pine forests, and greenways, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Villarejo de Salvanés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 140 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villarejo de Salvanés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Villarejo de Salvanés?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Villarejo de Salvanés, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult trails to explore the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from picturesque hills and valleys to extensive farmlands. You'll cycle through vast olive groves and pine forests, including the natural Aleppo pine forest of Valdepuerco. Many routes utilize unpaved roads and dedicated greenways, providing a varied and scenic experience.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes. The region's greenways, such as sections of the Tajuña Greenway, are generally flat and well-suited for a relaxed ride. These paths often traverse cultivated fields and offer pleasant scenery without significant elevation changes.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit while cycling around Villarejo de Salvanés?
A: The area is rich in history. You can explore the &lt;strong&gt;Castle of Villarejo de Salvanés&lt;/strong&gt; (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Villarejo de Salvanés&lt;/a&gt;) and the charming town of Chinchón, known for its picturesque main square and the &lt;strong&gt;Chinchón Castle&lt;/strong&gt; (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6286138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón Castle&lt;/a&gt;). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962347346/route-of-the-castles" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Castles&lt;/a&gt; specifically allows exploration of historical sites, including &lt;strong&gt;Fuentidueña de Tajo Town Center and Historic Landmarks&lt;/strong&gt; (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6439192" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentidueña de Tajo Town Center and Historic Landmarks&lt;/a&gt;).

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Villarejo de Salvanés area?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33702455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña Greenway – Cantarranas Mill Hotel loop from Villarejo de Salvanés&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 53.6 km circular path. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6911521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chinchón – Chinchón Castle loop from Villarejo de Salvanés&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 53.8 km long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Villarejo de Salvanés?
A: The region is popular for cycling in January and September, suggesting that spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for exploring. These seasons typically avoid the extreme heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter, providing ideal cycling weather.

Q: What are the natural features I might encounter on the routes?
A: You'll find a variety of natural features, including extensive olive groves, pine forests like Valdepuerco, and the scenic Tajuña Greenway. This greenway passes through a magnificent regional park around the lower courses of the Manzanares and Jarama rivers, where you might observe wetlands and various bird species.

Q: Are there any routes that follow greenways or dedicated cycling paths?
A: Absolutely. The **Tajuña Greenway (Vía Verde del Tajuña)** is a significant feature, offering an easy route suitable for cycling. It connects towns and traverses moors, wetlands, and cultivated fields. This greenway also links with the 'Tren de los 40 Días' Greenway, expanding the network of dedicated cycling paths.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Villarejo de Salvanés?
A: The routes in Villarejo de Salvanés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of historical sites, and the well-maintained greenways that make for an enjoyable touring experience.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views of the Madrid mountains?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning panoramic views, including glimpses of the Madrid mountains. The varied terrain, with its picturesque hills and valleys, ensures many opportunities to enjoy expansive vistas as you cycle through the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Rolling Hills – Shop loop from Villarejo de Salvanés&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option for such views.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20145474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morata de Tajuña Town Square – View of Morata de Tajuña loop from Villarejo de Salvanés&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 56.36 km route with significant elevation gain, offering a rigorous cycling experience.
tips: It is part of the old architectural complex of the city fortress and dates from the Middle Ages.

The Fuentidueña de Tajo bridge is an engineering work from the mid-19th century that restored the passage through this river on the way to Cuenca. It is the oldest large metal bridge in Madrid.
This work allowed communications to be modernized at the time of its execution.

Fuentidueña de Tajo is a beautiful town located in a depression in the Madrid region of Las Vegas. Its town center has an interesting historical heritage, such as the ruins of the castle of Santiago, the clock tower or the church of San Andrés Apóstol.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Route of the Castles?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 40.7 kilometers long. Cyclists can expect to complete it in about 2 hours and 58 minutes to 4 hours, depending on their pace and stops to explore the historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this gravel route?
faqs.answer: The terrain consists primarily of gravel paths and forest tracks, typical of 'caminos y pistas forestales' in the region. It features pleasant descents and ascents that are generally not very pronounced, making it suitable for gravel bikes. While mostly unpaved, it's designed for cycling tourism.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Castles suitable for beginner gravel cyclists?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as having a moderate difficulty level. While not overly technical, it's best suited for cyclists who have some experience on mixed-surface terrain and are in good physical condition. Absolute beginners might find some sections challenging.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in historical significance. You can visit the unique Castle of Villarejo de Salvanés, explore the town of Villamanrique del Tajo with its 17th-century Palace of Buenamesón, and discover the freely accessible Castle of Fuentidueña de Tajo. You'll also pass the impressive Iron Bridge of Fuentidueña de Tajo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: As the route is circular, it conveniently starts and ends in Villarejo de Salvanés. You can find parking options within the town to begin your cycling adventure.

faqs.question: Is the Route of the Castles dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail itself does not have specific restrictions against dogs. However, when cycling through towns or visiting historical sites, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of local regulations and other visitors. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and can handle the cycling distance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To enjoy the route comfortably, the best times to visit are generally spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder temperatures of winter, making for a more pleasant cycling experience.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay entrance fees for the trail?
faqs.answer: There are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Route of the Castles itself. While the Castle of Fuentidueña de Tajo is freely accessible, some specific parts of other historical sites, like the restored tower of tribute at the Castle of Villarejo de Salvanés, might have separate entry fees if you wish to go inside.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other official trails. You will cycle along parts of the Camino Natural del Tajo, including segments of the Camino Natural del Tajo - GR 113, and the Cañada Real Soriana Oriental.

faqs.question: Are there any places to stop for refreshments or food along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through towns like Villarejo de Salvanés and Fuentidueña del Tajo. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, or shops where you can refuel and take a break during your ride.

faqs.question: What is a good insider tip for navigating this route?
faqs.answer: For convenient navigation, digital tracks in GPX or KMZ format are available for download. It's highly recommended to download these to your cycling GPS device or smartphone before you start, as this will help you stay on track and make the most of your historical cycling tour.

waypoints.name: Iron Bridge of Fuentidueña de Tajo
